dependencies.description:
A Projector Augmented Wave (PAW) code for generating atom-centered
functions. Official website: http://pwpaw.wfu.edu User's guide:
~/doc/atompaw-usersguide.pdf
dependencies.name: atompaw

dependencies.description:
Lightweight but flexible shim designed to rectify the incompatibilities
between the Accelerate/vecLib BLAS and LAPACK libraries shipped with
macOS and FORTRAN code compiled with modern compilers such as GNU
Fortran.
dependencies.name: blas

dependencies.description:
The NVIDIA HPC SDK is a comprehensive suite of compilers, libraries and
tools essential to maximizing developer productivity and the performance
and portability of HPC applications. The NVIDIA HPC SDK C, C++, and
Fortran compilers support GPU acceleration of HPC modeling and
simulation applications with standard C++ and Fortran, OpenACC
directives, and CUDA. GPU-accelerated math libraries maximize
performance on common HPC algorithms, and optimized communications
libraries enable standards-based multi-GPU and scalable systems
programming. Performance profiling and debugging tools simplify porting
and optimization of HPC applications.
dependencies.name: c

dependencies.description:
CUDA is a parallel computing platform and programming model invented by
NVIDIA. It enables dramatic increases in computing performance by
harnessing the power of the graphics processing unit (GPU). Note: This
package does not currently install the drivers necessary to run CUDA.
These will need to be installed manually. See:
https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/ for details.
dependencies.name: cuda

dependencies.description:
The NVIDIA HPC SDK is a comprehensive suite of compilers, libraries and
tools essential to maximizing developer productivity and the performance
and portability of HPC applications. The NVIDIA HPC SDK C, C++, and
Fortran compilers support GPU acceleration of HPC modeling and
simulation applications with standard C++ and Fortran, OpenACC
directives, and CUDA. GPU-accelerated math libraries maximize
performance on common HPC algorithms, and optimized communications
libraries enable standards-based multi-GPU and scalable systems
programming. Performance profiling and debugging tools simplify porting
and optimization of HPC applications.
dependencies.name: cxx

dependencies.description:
NVPL FFT (NVIDIA Performance Libraries FFT) is part of NVIDIA
Performance Libraries and provides Fast Fourier Transform (FFT)
calculations on ARM CPUs.
dependencies.name: fftw-api

dependencies.description:
The NVIDIA HPC SDK is a comprehensive suite of compilers, libraries and
tools essential to maximizing developer productivity and the performance
and portability of HPC applications. The NVIDIA HPC SDK C, C++, and
Fortran compilers support GPU acceleration of HPC modeling and
simulation applications with standard C++ and Fortran, OpenACC

performance on common HPC algorithms, and optimized communications

and optimization of HPC applications.
dependencies.name: fortran

dependencies.description:
GNU Make is a tool which controls the generation of executables and
other non-source files of a program from the program's source files.
dependencies.name: gmake

dependencies.description:
The GNU config.guess and config.sub scripts versioned by timestamp.
This package can be used as a build dependency for autotools packages
that ship a tarball with outdated config.guess and config.sub files.
dependencies.name: gnuconfig

dependencies.description:
HDF5 is a data model, library, and file format for storing and managing
data. It supports an unlimited variety of datatypes, and is designed for
flexible and efficient I/O and for high volume and complex data.
dependencies.name: hdf5

dependencies.description:
HIP is a C++ Runtime API and Kernel Language that allows developers to
create portable applications for AMD and NVIDIA GPUs from single source
code.
dependencies.name: hip

dependencies.description: hipBLAS is a BLAS marshalling library, with multiple supported backends
dependencies.name: hipblas

dependencies.description:
hipFFT is an FFT marshalling library. Currently, hipFFT supports either
rocFFT or cuFFT as backends.hipFFT exports an interface that does not
require the client to change, regardless of the chosen backend. It sits
between the application and the backend FFT library, marshalling inputs
into the backend and results back to the application.
dependencies.name: hipfft

dependencies.description:
hipSOLVER is a LAPACK marshalling library, with multiple supported
backends. It sits between the application and a 'worker' LAPACK library,
marshalling inputs into the backend library and marshalling results back
to the application. hipSOLVER exports an interface that does not require
the client to change, regardless of the chosen backend. Currently,
hipSOLVER supports rocSOLVER and cuSOLVER as backends.
dependencies.name: hipsolver

dependencies.description:
This repository includes the user mode API nterfaces and libraries
necessary for host applications to launch computer kernels to available
HSA ROCm kernel agents.AMD Heterogeneous System Architecture HSA - Linux
HSA Runtime for Boltzmann (ROCm) platforms.
dependencies.name: hsa-rocr-dev

dependencies.description:
Lightweight but flexible shim designed to rectify the incompatibilities
between the Accelerate/vecLib BLAS and LAPACK libraries shipped with
macOS and FORTRAN code compiled with modern compilers such as GNU
Fortran.
dependencies.name: lapack

dependencies.description:
Libxc is a library of exchange-correlation functionals for density-
functional theory.
dependencies.name: libxc

dependencies.description:
Libxml2 is the XML C parser and toolkit developed for the Gnome project
(but usable outside of the Gnome platform), it is free software
available under the MIT License.
dependencies.name: libxml2

dependencies.description:
Toolkit for the construction of highly optimized compilers, optimizers,
and run-time environments.
dependencies.name: llvm-amdgpu

dependencies.description: IBM MPI implementation from Spectrum MPI.
dependencies.name: mpi

dependencies.description:
NetCDF (network Common Data Form) is a set of software libraries and
machine-independent data formats that support the creation, access, and
sharing of array-oriented scientific data. This is the C distribution.
dependencies.name: netcdf-c

dependencies.description:
NetCDF (network Common Data Form) is a set of software libraries and
machine-independent data formats that support the creation, access, and
sharing of array-oriented scientific data. This is the Fortran
distribution.
dependencies.name: netcdf-fortran

dependencies.description: NVPL ScaLAPACK (NVIDIA Performance Libraries ScaLAPACK).
dependencies.name: scalapack

dependencies.description:
Wannier90 calculates maximally-localised Wannier functions (MLWFs).
Wannier90 is released under the GNU General Public License.
dependencies.name: wannier90

description:
ABINIT is a package whose main program allows one to find the total
energy, charge density and electronic structure of systems made of
electrons and nuclei (molecules and periodic solids) within Density
Functional Theory (DFT), using pseudopotentials and a planewave or
wavelet basis. ABINIT also includes options to optimize the geometry
according to the DFT forces and stresses, or to perform molecular
dynamics simulations using these forces, or to generate dynamical
matrices, Born effective charges, and dielectric tensors, based on
Density-Functional Perturbation Theory, and many more properties.
Excited states can be computed within the Many-Body Perturbation Theory
(the GW approximation and the Bethe-Salpeter equation), and Time-
Dependent Density Functional Theory (for molecules). In addition to the
main ABINIT code, different utility programs are provided.
name: abinit

variants.description: Build systems supported by the package
variants.name: build_system

variants.description: Enable GPU markers, either NVTX for CUDA or rocTX for ROCM
variants.name: gpu_markers

variants.description: Enable OpenMP offload support. Requires CUDA & NVHPC or ROCM & Cray CE
variants.name: gpu_openmp_offload

variants.description: Enable libxml2 support, used by multibinit
variants.name: libxml2

variants.description: Builds with MPI support. Requires MPI2+
variants.name: mpi

variants.description: Enables OpenMP threads. Use threaded FFTW3
variants.name: openmp

variants.description: Select the optimization flavor to use.
variants.name: optimization-flavor
